data.title: MRPeasy Tips: Best Ways to Manage Small Manufacturing
data.excerpt: Discover the best ways to use MRPeasy for inventory, production, and purchasing. See why 2000+ small manufacturers trust it daily.

$media.alt: MRPeasy Tips: Best Ways to Manage Small Manufacturing

children.text: Running a small manufacturing business means juggling production schedules, raw material orders, warehouse counts, and customer invoices — often across spreadsheets and sticky notes. MRPeasy fixes that by pulling everything into a single cloud-based system that actually makes sense for teams with 10 to 200 employees. No bloated enterprise modules you will never open. No six-figure implementation fees. Just a tool built for the way small manufacturers actually work.

I have spent real time inside MRPeasy, and below I am sharing the tips and best practices that get the most out of it. Whether you are evaluating it for the first time or looking to squeeze more value from your current setup, this guide is for you.
Why MRPeasy Deserves a Spot on Your Shortlist

Most manufacturing ERPs fall into two camps: cheap but barely functional, or powerful but priced and sized for Fortune 500 plants. MRPeasy lives in the sweet spot that used to not exist. It is serious software — MRP logic, bill-of-materials management, lot traceability, procurement automation — wrapped in an interface that a production manager can learn in days instead of months.
Here is what stands out immediately:
You do not need an IT department to run it.
The cloud setup means no servers, no installs, no version headaches.
Pricing starts at $49 per user per month
(Starter plan), which is remarkable for software that includes production planning, BOM management, warehouse tracking, CRM, and standard accounting.
Integrations are practical, not decorative.
QuickBooks Online, Xero, Shopify, Magento, BigCommerce, WooCommerce, Pipedrive, ShipStation, and Zapier all connect natively. You are not duct-tiping things together with custom scripts.
If you want to see it in action before committing,
grab the free trial here
. There is no credit card required, and you get enough runway to set up a real pilot with your own products and processes.
For a deeper dive into what makes it different from generic project tools, check out
what MRPeasy actually is
How MRPeasy Keeps Production Running Smoothly

The core of MRPeasy is its MRP engine — the brain that calculates what materials you need, when you need them, and how to schedule your shop floor so nothing bottlenecks. But the tips below go beyond the obvious.
Tip 1: Start with your BOMs, not your inventory.

A common mistake is importing all inventory first. Resist that urge. Build out your Bills of Materials accurately, and MRPeasy will tell you what raw materials and sub-assemblies you actually need. This one shift saves weeks of cleanup later.
Tip 2: Use drag-and-drop rescheduling religiously.

Production does not go as planned — machines break, shipments arrive late, a rush order lands. MRPeasy lets you drag production orders to new dates and the system recalculates downstream dependencies automatically. Do not fight it; use it daily.
Tip 3: Turn on lot traceability from day one.

Even if you do not think you need it now, lot tracking pays off the moment a customer asks "which batch was this from?" or an auditor walks through the door. One user on G2 noted that their ISO auditors were impressed with how everything was documented inside MRPeasy — no extra systems needed.
Tip 4: Use the Internet-Kiosk for shop floor reporting.

Set up a cheap tablet at each workstation. Workers log time and mark steps complete right in MRPeasy through the kiosk view. This gives you real-time visibility into production progress without anyone filling out paper travelers that get lost by lunchtime.
Tip 5: Connect your sales channel early.

Whether you sell on Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, or BigCommerce, syncing orders into MRPeasy means your production team sees incoming demand without anyone re-keying data. It also means finished goods inventory updates automatically once production completes, so your storefront never oversells.
For a full walkthrough, I wrote a
step-by-step MRPeasy tutorial
that covers setup from zero to your first finished production order.
New and Standout Features Worth Exploring
MRPeasy is not static — the team ships updates that respond directly to what their manufacturing customers ask for. A few highlights worth your attention:
AI-assisted production planning.

The software now uses intelligent scheduling logic that considers machine capacity, worker availability, and material lead times simultaneously. It surfaces scheduling conflicts before they become shop-floor emergencies.
Multi-currency and multi-language support.
If you source materials from overseas or sell to international distributors, MRPeasy handles currency conversions and localized interfaces natively. No workarounds.
Integrated standard accounting.

You do not necessarily need to bolt on a separate accounting tool. The built-in module covers invoicing, purchase orders, cost tracking, and financial reporting. For companies already on QuickBooks or Xero, the sync keeps both systems in lockstep without double entry.
Supply Chain Management (SCM).
Purchase order suggestions based on actual demand — not gut feelings. The system calculates reorder points, lead times, and batch sizes, then hands you a ready-to-send PO list.
Warehouse Management System (WMS).
Manage multiple warehouse locations, track bin-level storage, and run inventory counts without a separate WMS platform.
Staying on top of new releases is easier than you think — here is our running list of
MRPeasy new features and updates
Who MRPeasy Is Built For
Not every tool is for everyone, and MRPeasy is refreshingly honest about its sweet spot. Here is who gets the most value:
Small manufacturers with 10–200 employees.

If you are past the spreadsheet stage but not ready (or willing) to spend $100K+ on SAP or Oracle, MRPeasy is the middle ground that actually works. Multiple G2 reviewers call it the best value in the small manufacturing space.
Make-to-order and make-to-stock producers.
Whether you build custom furniture one piece at a time or manufacture 10,000 widgets per batch on a repeating schedule, the MRP logic adapts.
Distributors who handle light assembly or kitting.
If you repackage, label, or assemble components before shipping, MRPeasy treats those as production orders and tracks them properly.
Companies pursuing ISO or quality certifications.
The built-in lot traceability, documented processes, and audit trail features mean you are halfway through compliance requirements just by using the system day to day.
Teams that sell online.

The Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, and BigCommerce integrations make MRPeasy a natural fit for e-commerce brands that manufacture their own products. Orders flow in; inventory flows out; nobody manually enters anything.

If you are comparing MRPeasy against project-focused tools like ClickUp or monday.com, keep in mind those are great for task management but lack manufacturing-specific logic like BOM explosion, material requirements planning, and shop floor scheduling. For a detailed comparison of when to use what, see our guide on
choosing MRPeasy over alternatives
Real Wins From the Shop Floor
Theory is nice. Results are better. Here are patterns I see repeated across MRPeasy reviews and case studies:
Doubled capacity without hiring.

One manufacturer reported that streamlining production and procurement with MRPeasy freed up so much time that their effective capacity doubled. The owner now spends hours per week on growth strategy instead of firefighting orders.
ISO audit passed with flying colors.

A company going through ISO certification found that MRPeasy documented everything auditors needed — lot numbers, process steps, supplier records — all in one place. No binder full of printouts required.
Bug-free and reliable.
A reviewer with 15 years in the software industry called MRPeasy notably bug-free, better than the industry average. For a tool that touches daily production, that reliability matters enormously.
Fast onboarding.
Teams consistently report going live within weeks, not months. The interface is intuitive enough that shop floor workers adopt it without major resistance — a bigger hurdle than most people realize.
Want to see more use cases across different industries? Browse our collection of
MRPeasy use cases and examples
Ready to test it yourself?
Start your free MRPeasy trial here
and see how it fits your operation in real conditions.
Getting the Most Out of Your Trial
A few final tips to make your trial period count:
Import real data, not test data.
Use your actual product catalog, real BOMs, and current supplier list. Simulated data tells you nothing about whether the software fits your workflow.
Run a full production cycle.
Create a sales order, let MRPeasy generate production and purchase orders, receive materials, complete production, and ship. One complete loop reveals more than ten hours of clicking around.
Invite your team early.
Production managers, warehouse staff, and purchasing leads should all log in during the trial. Their feedback on usability will tell you more than any feature checklist.
Use the integrations.
Connect your accounting platform and e-commerce store during the trial so you can evaluate the full data flow, not just isolated modules.

Frequently Asked Questions
Is MRPeasy really free to try?
Yes. MRPeasy offers a free trial with no credit card required. You get access to the full feature set of your chosen plan so you can test it with real data before committing.
Can MRPeasy replace QuickBooks?

MRPeasy includes standard accounting features that work well for many small manufacturers. However, if you prefer to keep QuickBooks or Xero, the native integration syncs data seamlessly, so you can use both without double entry.
How long does it take to get MRPeasy running?

Most small manufacturers go live within two to four weeks, depending on the complexity of their product catalog and how quickly they can gather BOM and supplier data. The interface is designed for fast adoption, so training time is minimal compared to legacy ERP systems.

children.text: What size company is MRPeasy best suited for?

MRPeasy is designed specifically for small manufacturers and distributors with 10 to 200 employees. It fills the gap between basic spreadsheets and expensive enterprise ERP systems, offering serious MRP functionality at a price point that makes sense for growing businesses.
Does MRPeasy integrate with Shopify and other e-commerce platforms?

Yes. MRPeasy integrates natively with Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, and BigCommerce. Orders sync automatically into MRPeasy for production planning, and finished goods inventory updates back to your storefront in real time to prevent overselling.
Is MRPeasy suitable for companies that need ISO or quality certification?

Absolutely. MRPeasy includes built-in lot traceability, documented process steps, and complete audit trails. Customers pursuing ISO certification have reported that auditors were impressed with how thoroughly the system documents manufacturing processes without needing additional software.
